findings.message: Root marketplace plugin.json declares version '1.0.0' while plugins/visual-explainer/.claude-plugin/plugin.json declares '0.6.3'. If the wrapper is expected to mirror the plugin version, this is stale.
git clone fetches and installs external code from GitHub at runtime; supply-chain compromise of the upstream repo would silently deliver malicious content to all installers.
rm -rf on $HOME/.pi/agent/skills/visual-explainer removes an entire directory tree on each reinstall; path is hardcoded so risk is limited, but accidental data loss is possible.
sed uses | as delimiter while interpolating $SKILL_DIR (derived from $HOME); if $HOME contains |, the sed expression breaks silently.

'key' used ~6 times as a vague filter with no concrete threshold: 'key snippets', 'key metrics', 'key invariants', 'key function names', 'key implementation details', 'key decisions'. Each occurrence leaves qualification criteria undefined for the agent.
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter; tools used (Bash for git/gh, Read) are implicit.

Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (line 4), 'significant areas of change' (line 38), 'significant design choice' (line 48), 'major feature comparisons' (line 38). Each leaves scope undefined for the agent.
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter; tools used (Bash, Read, Edit) are implicit.
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
Command takes $@ but provides no fallback behavior when invoked with no arguments; the prompt 'generate a slide deck for: ' is undefined.

Command has 4+ sequential phases (read references, choose aesthetic, plan narrative arc, generate HTML, write file) described in bold paragraph headers but not numbered; the overall workflow structure is implicit.
Vague qualifiers: 'stunning' (description), 'distinctive direction' (line 10), 'compelling' (line 14). These are aesthetic adjectives with no measurable threshold.
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
Command takes $@ but provides no fallback behavior when invoked with no arguments.
Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual implementation plan' (line 4), 'relevant codebase' (line 16), 'similar features' (line 27).
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
Command takes $@ but provides no fallback behavior when invoked with no arguments.
Vague qualifiers: 'beautiful standalone HTML diagram' (description), 'distinctive aesthetic' (line 7), 'genuinely enhance the page' (line 8).
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
$1 (plan file path) is required but there is no fallback when the argument is omitted.
Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual plan review' (line 4), 'significant area of concern' (line 64).
Command YAML frontmatter is missing the required 'name' field; only 'description' is present.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
Vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive visual project recap' (line 4), 'concise note' (line 54), 'key source files' (line 23).
share.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all — the 'name' field is missing. The file is formatted as README documentation rather than a command template.
share.md has no YAML frontmatter block at all — the 'description' field is missing. Without frontmatter, the command will not register correctly.
Command does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field in frontmatter.
$1 (file-path) is listed as required with no fallback clause in the command template.

share.md is formatted as README documentation (markdown headers, code fences, sections) rather than a command template (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). Every other command in the plugin follows the frontmatter template convention.
Executes an external vercel-deploy script that publishes the user's HTML file to a public URL without a pre-deploy warning about data exposure.

Root marketplace plugin.json declares version '1.0.0' while plugins/visual-explainer/.claude-plugin/plugin.json declares '0.6.3'. If the wrapper is expected to mirror the plugin version, this is stale.

share.md is formatted as README documentation (markdown headers, code fences, sections) rather than a command template (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). Every other command in the plugin follows the frontmatter template convention.
